global.title: The bridge pattern lets a single PGlite serve both Switchboard GQL remotes and Swarm feeds transparently via independent cursors.
global.content:
## GQL + Swarm Dual Sync

In one browser, PGlite acts as the hub between two worlds:

- **Switchboard world**: remote drives pulled in via GQL channels.
- **Swarm world**: drives and documents mirrored out to encrypted feeds.

Each drive is independent:

- Drive A: pulled from Switchboard (GQL) **and** pushed to Swarm Feed A.
- Drive B: same dual path.
- Drive C: locally created by the user, only pushed to Swarm Feed C (no Switchboard counterpart).

### Why it works

Both channels read from the same `operation_index_operations` table, where every op gets a global ordinal. Each remote has an independent cursor in `sync_cursors`, so progress on one channel never interferes with the other.

`SyncManager` orchestrates both transparently — it does not know whether a given remote is GQL or Swarm, it just pumps operations through `channel.outbox` / `channel.inbox` / `channel.deadLetter`.

This is the foundation that lets Connect work decentralized-first while retaining compatibility with Switchboard-hosted drives.
global.description: Operation_index_operations is shared by both channels; each remote tracks its own cursor in sync_cursors so GQL pull and Swarm push run in parallel without interference.
